preview_text:
The model favors Spain heavily at 75%, leaving Belgium just 4% to win outright. That 71-percentage-point gap reflects a stark asymmetry: the model sees Spain as commanding favorites, while Belgium's upset probability sits near zero by this measure.

The spread reveals how the model constructs that dominance. Spain's 75% win rate and 20% draw rate combine to give it a 95% chance of avoiding defeat. Belgium's path runs almost entirely through a draw, which accounts for most of its 24% not-a-loss scenario. A decisive contest favoring one side appears most likely under the model, but a stalemate remains plausible at roughly one-in-five odds.

The model and sportsbooks diverge sharply. Books price Spain at 58%, some 17 percentage points below the model's 75%, and they assign Belgium 17%—more than four times the model's 4%. Polymarket pricing aligns closer to sportsbooks than to the model. This dashboard presents both views without adjudicating which better reflects match reality.
